title: Build a Full Website Page
summary: Combine HTML structure, responsive CSS layout, media, forms, and JavaScript into one complete page.

guide.title: Capstone checklist
guide.intro: You have learned the building blocks for a modern website. This lesson brings them together in one page you can copy to your own index.html folder.

steps.title: Layout shell
steps.text: Run Example 1 to see navbar, sidebar, main content, and footer with responsive CSS.

steps.title: Mobile menu
steps.text: Run Example 2 and click Menu on a narrow screen to open the sidebar.

steps.title: Make it yours
steps.text: Use the practice lab to change the site name, links, and add a video or contact form section.

steps.title: Publish
steps.text: Save as index.html in your project folder. Open in a browser locally, or upload to GitHub Pages to share online.

guide.footer: Congratulations—you now have the skills to build and customize a full static website.

examples.title: Full page layout (HTML + CSS)
examples.description: Purpose: This is the finished layout shell every modern site starts from—navigation, sidebar, content, and footer in one responsive page. Output: A complete light-themed page with top nav, side links, welcome text, and footer; sidebar stacks on small screens. Try: Change the site name in the header or add a fourth sidebar link.

files.html:

Welcome

Getting started

Edit this page in your text editor and refresh the browser to see changes.

© 2026 DevLearn — built with HTML & CSS

examples.title: Full page with mobile menu (HTML + CSS + JS)
examples.description: Purpose: Real websites hide the sidebar on phones until the user taps a menu button—this example adds that behavior on top of the full layout. Output: Same page as Example 1, plus a Menu button that opens and closes the sidebar on narrow screens. Try: Resize the preview or use mobile view in DevTools, then click Menu.

practice.description: Customize the capstone page: change the site name, update sidebar links, and add one new section—a short contact form, a video tag, or an extra content card in main.
